# CORNET Unified Config Schema Reference

This page is auto-generated from `cornet/config/schema.py`.
All fields support YAML and JSON input.
Dot-path notation (e.g. `network.middleware.rtf`) is used in sweep axes.

---

## UnifiedConfig

Root configuration model. Every `config.yaml` must parse as `UnifiedConfig`.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `network` | [NetworkConfig](#networkconfig) | â€” | âś“ | Network simulation backend configuration (NS-3, Mininet, or both). |
| `robot` | [RobotConfig](#robotconfig) | â€” | âś“ | Robot simulation backend configuration (Gazebo + ROS 2). |
| `experiment` | [ExperimentConfig](#experimentconfig) | â€” | âś“ | Experiment runtime settings: duration, output, metrics, and optional parameter sweep. |

## NetworkConfig

Network simulation backend settings (`network:` section).

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `plugin` | string | â€” | âś“ | Network backend plugin identifier: 'ns3', 'mininet', or 'ns3+mininet'. |
| `type` | string | â€” | âś“ | Network simulation type. Must match plugin. |
| `nodes` | list[[NodeConfig](#nodeconfig)] | `[]` |  | List of network nodes in the topology. |
| `mininet` | [MininetConfig](#mininetconfig) | null | null |  | Mininet-WiFi configuration. Required when type includes 'mininet'. |
| `middleware` | [MiddlewareConfig](#middlewareconfig) | null | null |  | Co-simulation middleware settings (TUN bridge, packet dispatcher, AoI tracker). |
| `mobility` | [MobilityConfig](#mobilityconfig) | null | null |  | Live position update settings (PositionBroadcaster from Gazebo to NS-3). |
| `scenario` | [ScenarioConfig](#scenarioconfig) | null | null |  | 5G/6G NS-3 scenario profile. Required when type includes 'ns3'. |

## NodeConfig

A single network node in the topology.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | â€” | âś“ | Unique node identifier within the network topology. |
| `type` | string | `"MOBILE"` |  | Node role. MOBILE/STATIC for Mininet nodes; UE/GNB/EPC for NS-3 5G nodes. |
| `container` | [ContainerConfig](#containerconfig) | null | null |  | Docker container config for this node. None = bare Mininet host. |
| `ip` | string | null | null |  | Static IP address (CIDR or bare, e.g. '10.0.0.1/24'). None = assigned by network plugin. |
| `x` | number | null | null |  | Initial X position in metres (simulation world frame). |
| `y` | number | null | null |  | Initial Y position in metres (simulation world frame). |
| `z` | number | null | null |  | Initial Z position in metres (simulation world frame, 0 = ground level). |
| `model_name` | string | null | null |  | Gazebo model name to track for live position updates via PositionBroadcaster. |

## ContainerConfig

Docker container configuration for a network node.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `image` | string | â€” | âś“ | Docker image name (e.g. 'ros:humble' or a custom image built from the task's Dockerfile). |
| `environment` | dict[str, string] | `{}` |  | Environment variables injected into the container at start-up. |
| `volumes` | list[string] | `[]` |  | Host-to-container volume mounts in 'host:container[:mode]' format. |
| `cpu_quota` | number | null | null |  | CPU quota as a fraction of one core (e.g. 0.5 = 50% of one CPU). None = unlimited. |
| `mem_limit_mb` | integer | null | null |  | Container memory limit in MiB. None = unlimited. |

## MininetConfig

Mininet-WiFi access point settings.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `wmediumd` | boolean | `False` |  | Enable wmediumd wireless medium daemon for realistic Wi-Fi interference emulation (requires Docker). |
| `ssid` | string | `"cornet-net"` |  | SSID for the Mininet-WiFi access point. |
| `mode` | string | `"g"` |  | IEEE 802.11 mode ('a', 'b', 'g', 'n', 'ac'). |
| `channel` | integer | `1` |  | Wi-Fi channel number (1â€“14 for 2.4 GHz, 36â€“165 for 5 GHz). |

## MiddlewareConfig

Co-simulation middleware layer settings.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enabled` | boolean | `False` |  | Enable the CORNET co-simulation middleware layer (TUN manager, packet dispatcher, AoI tracker, physics clock). |
| `ip_list` | list[string] | `[]` |  | IP addresses of simulation nodes that the TUN manager should bridge to the robot network. |
| `rtf` | number | `1.0` |  | Real-time factor. 1.0 = wall-clock speed. Values < 1 slow simulation; 0 = maximum simulation speed (decoupled from wall clock). |
| `deadline_s` | number | `0.5` |  | Packet age deadline in seconds. Packets older than this are dropped by the dispatcher before delivery. |
| `ber` | number | `0.0` |  | Bit-error rate in [0, 1]. 0 = no artificial errors. Applied by the dispatcher before AoI accounting. |
| `clock_timeout_s` | number | `5.0` |  | Seconds after which the physics clock falls back to wall clock if no NS-3 clock tick is received. |
| `clock_socket` | string | `"/tmp/cornet_clock.sock"` |  | UNIX socket path for the physics clock synchronisation channel. |
| `positions_socket` | string | `"/tmp/cornet_positions.sock"` |  | UNIX socket path for the PositionBroadcaster live position feed. |

## MobilityConfig

Live position update settings for PositionBroadcaster.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enabled` | boolean | `False` |  | Enable the PositionBroadcaster, which pushes Gazebo model poses to NS-3 node positions in real time. |
| `source` | string | `"socket"` |  | Position data source: 'socket' (CORNET middleware), 'ros2_topic' (ROS 2 /tf), or 'none' (static positions only). |
| `update_hz` | number | `10.0` |  | Position update rate in Hz when update_mode='periodic'. |
| `update_mode` | string | `"periodic"` |  | When to push position updates: 'periodic' (every 1/update_hz s), 'threshold' (when movement exceeds position_threshold_m), 'step_aligned' (on NS-3 simulation step boundaries). |
| `position_threshold_m` | number | `0.5` |  | Minimum Euclidean movement in metres that triggers a position update when update_mode='threshold'. |

## ScenarioConfig

5G/6G NS-3 scenario profile selection.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `profile` | string | â€” | âś“ | NS-3 scenario profile to run. One of: 5g_nr_urllc, 5g_nr_embb, 5g_nr_mmtc, 6g_thz. |
| `numerology` | integer | null | null |  | 5G NR numerology index (0â€“4). Controls subcarrier spacing: 0=15kHz, 1=30kHz, 2=60kHz, 3=120kHz, 4=240kHz. None = scenario default. |
| `bandwidth_mhz` | number | null | null |  | Channel bandwidth in MHz (e.g. 20, 40, 100). None = scenario default. |
| `scheduler` | string | null | null |  | NR MAC scheduler override: 'ofdma-rr', 'ofdma-pf', 'ofdma-edf', 'ofdma-aoi'. None = scenario default. |
| `experimental` | boolean | `False` |  | Automatically set to True for profiles that are not production-ready (e.g. 6g_thz). Do not set manually. |

## RobotConfig

Robot simulation backend settings (`robot:` section).

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `plugin` | string | â€” | âś“ | Robot simulation backend plugin identifier (e.g. 'gazebo'). |
| `launch_file` | string | null | null |  | Path to a ROS 2 launch file to use instead of auto-generated launch. None = Gazebo plugin auto-generates. |
| `world` | string | null | null |  | Path to the Gazebo world file (.sdf or .world). None = Gazebo plugin auto-generates an empty world. |
| `robots` | list[[RobotEntry](#robotentry)] | `[]` |  | List of robots to spawn in the simulation world. |

## RobotEntry

A single robot to spawn in the simulation world.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | â€” | âś“ | Unique robot identifier within the simulation (used as ROS namespace and Gazebo model name). |
| `model` | [ModelConfig](#modelconfig) | â€” | âś“ | Robot model file reference (URDF or SDF). |
| `pose` | [PoseConfig](#poseconfig) | â€” |  | Initial spawn pose in the Gazebo world. |
| `ros_namespace` | string | null | null |  | ROS 2 namespace for this robot's topics and services. None = uses robot name. |

## ModelConfig

Robot model file reference.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | â€” | âś“ | Robot model format: 'urdf' (ROS 2) or 'sdf' (Gazebo/SDF). |
| `path` | string | â€” | âś“ | Path to the model file, relative to the task directory or absolute. |

## PoseConfig

Initial spawn pose for a robot.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `x` | number | `0.0` |  | X position in metres (world frame). |
| `y` | number | `0.0` |  | Y position in metres (world frame). |
| `z` | number | `0.0` |  | Z position in metres (world frame, 0 = ground level). |
| `roll` | number | `0.0` |  | Roll angle in radians. |
| `pitch` | number | `0.0` |  | Pitch angle in radians. |
| `yaw` | number | `0.0` |  | Yaw angle in radians (0 = facing +X axis). |

## ExperimentConfig

Experiment runtime settings (`experiment:` section).

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | â€” | âś“ | Human-readable experiment name, used as the leaderboard title. |
| `duration` | number | â€” | âś“ | Experiment wall-clock duration in seconds. The orchestrator terminates all plugins after this time. |
| `output_dir` | string | `"results"` |  | Directory where results, logs, and leaderboard entries are written (relative to task directory or absolute). |
| `primary_metric` | string | null | null |  | Key from EvalTool output to rank leaderboard entries by. None = leaderboard is unranked. |
| `higher_is_better` | boolean | `False` |  | Leaderboard sort direction for primary_metric. True = higher score ranks first; False = lower score ranks first. |
| `sweep` | [SweepConfig](#sweepconfig) | null | null |  | Parameter sweep configuration. When set, the orchestrator expands variants and runs each independently. |

## SweepConfig

Parameter sweep configuration.

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `axes` | dict[str, list[any]] | â€” | âś“ | Parameter sweep axes as a mapping of dot-path config keys to lists of values (e.g. {'network.numerology': [1, 2, 3], 'network.bandwidth_mhz': [20, 40]}). The cartesian product of all axes is expanded into one variant per combination. |
| `parallel` | boolean | `False` |  | Run sweep variants in parallel subprocesses. False = sequential (safer for resource-limited machines). |
| `repeats` | integer | `1` |  | Number of independent repetitions per variant (for statistical averaging). Must be >= 1. |
